content: Gold has a long-term correlation of -0.40 to -0.60 with the US Dollar Index (DXY) and +0.30 to +0.50 with CPI inflation. During periods of real interest rates below -1%, gold historically appreciates 15-25% annually. Central banks collectively hold ~36,000 tonnes of gold reserves.

OPEC+ controls approximately 40% of global crude oil production (~40 million barrels/day out of ~100 million total). A production cut of 1 million barrels/day typically increases WTI crude prices by $5-$15/barrel over 2-3 months, depending on demand elasticity and strategic reserve releases.

Copper is called 'Dr. Copper' because its price is a leading indicator of global economic health. Copper demand is ~70% industrial (construction 30%, electronics 25%, transport 15%). When copper prices rise >15% in a quarter, global GDP growth acceleration follows within 3-6 months with ~75% historical accuracy.

Contango occurs when futures price > spot price, common in storage-cost commodities like oil. The contango spread in WTI can reach $5-$10/barrel across 6 months during oversupply. Backwardation (spot > futures) signals immediate scarcity and is bullish for near-term prices, occurring ~30% of the time in oil markets.

Roll yield is the profit/loss from rolling expiring futures contracts to the next month. In contango, roll yield is negative (buy higher-priced next month). In backwardation, roll yield is positive. USO (oil ETF) lost ~40% to negative roll yield over 5 years during persistent contango from 2015-2020.

Natural gas has extreme seasonality: prices typically rise 20-40% from October to January as winter heating demand peaks (US consumption ~90 Bcf/day in winter vs ~60 Bcf/day in summer). The EIA weekly storage report (Thursday 10:30 AM EST) moves Henry Hub prices 2-5% on surprise draws/injections.

Agricultural commodities follow planting and harvest cycles. Corn and soybean prices typically peak in June-July during pollination risk and trough in October-November at harvest. The USDA WASDE report (monthly) provides supply/demand estimates that move grain prices 3-8% on surprise revisions to yield or acreage.

The gold/silver ratio averages 60-80 historically but can spike to 100+ during crises (it hit 125 in March 2020). When the ratio exceeds 80, mean-reversion traders go long silver/short gold. Silver is more volatile (~1.5x gold's daily range) due to its dual industrial/precious metal nature with ~50% industrial demand.

Industrial metals (copper, aluminum, zinc, nickel) are traded primarily on the London Metal Exchange (LME) with standardized 25-tonne contracts. Aluminum is the most traded by volume (~60 million tonnes/year). China consumes ~50% of global industrial metals, making Chinese PMI and construction starts critical demand indicators.

Soft commodities include coffee, cocoa, sugar, cotton, and orange juice, traded on ICE and CME. Arabica coffee prices can swing 30-50% in a year based on Brazilian frost risk (Brazil produces ~35% of global coffee). Cocoa prices spiked 200%+ in 2024 due to West African crop failures (Ivory Coast + Ghana = 60% of supply).

Commodity supercycles last 15-25 years, driven by structural demand shifts. The last supercycle (2000-2011) was driven by China's industrialization, with the CRB index rising ~170%. Key supercycle indicators: sustained demand growth >3% annually, capex underinvestment >5 years, inventory-to-consumption ratios at multi-decade lows.

WTI crude oil futures (CL) trade on CME with 1,000 barrel contracts ($1 move = $1,000 per contract). Brent-WTI spread reflects Atlantic Basin vs Gulf Coast supply dynamics, typically $2-$5/barrel with Brent premium. The spread widens when US production surges or Mideast geopolitical risk increases.

Gold futures (GC) on CME are 100 troy oz contracts ($1 move = $100 per contract). Margin requirement is typically $8,000-$12,000 (5-7% of contract value). Gold ETFs (GLD, IAU) hold physical gold in vaults; GLD holds ~900 tonnes, making it the 7th largest gold holder globally, bigger than most central banks.

Crude oil inventory data is released weekly: API on Tuesday (4:30 PM EST, unofficial) and EIA on Wednesday (10:30 AM EST, official). A draw of >5 million barrels vs expectations typically moves WTI 2-4%. The strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) holds ~370 million barrels (down from 727M peak in 2010).

Platinum/palladium are primarily industrial precious metals. Palladium demand is ~80% automotive catalytic converters (gasoline engines). The EV transition threatens palladium demand long-term as EVs need zero catalytic converters. Platinum is pivoting toward hydrogen fuel cell catalyst demand (~5-10g per fuel cell stack).

Commodity ETFs use three structures: physical-backed (GLD, SLV — hold metal), futures-based (USO, UNG — roll futures monthly), and equity-based (GDX, XLE — hold mining/energy stocks). Futures-based ETFs suffer roll yield decay of 5-15% annually in contango. Physical-backed ETFs track spot price with ~0.25-0.40% annual expense ratio.

Lithium carbonate prices swung from $6,000/tonne (2020) to $80,000/tonne (2022) back to $12,000/tonne (2024) due to EV demand boom then oversupply. Global lithium demand is projected to grow 25-30% annually through 2030. The lithium triangle (Chile, Argentina, Bolivia) holds ~55% of global reserves.

Heating degree days (HDD) and cooling degree days (CDD) drive natural gas and electricity demand. HDD = max(0, 65°F - avg daily temp). US Northeast winter with 10% above-normal HDDs can increase gas demand by 5-8 Bcf/day, often causing 10-20% price spikes in Henry Hub spot prices.

Iron ore is priced off the 62% Fe benchmark (Platts IODEX) delivered to Qingdao, China. Prices ranged $80-$220/tonne from 2020-2024. China's steel production (~1 billion tonnes/year, 54% of global output) is the dominant price driver. Port stockpiles above 140 million tonnes signal bearish oversupply.

Commodity trading advisors (CTAs) manage ~$400 billion using systematic trend-following strategies. CTAs typically use 20/50/200-day moving average crossovers and breakout systems. When CTA positioning in crude oil exceeds +3 standard deviations net long, a mean-reversion selloff of 10-15% occurs within 4-6 weeks ~65% of the time.

Uranium (U3O8) has a unique market structure: ~80% of demand is contracted long-term (10-15 year utility contracts) and ~20% trades on spot. Spot prices ranged $20-$50/lb from 2016-2022 then surged to $100+/lb in 2024. Global consumption is ~180 million lbs/year vs production of ~140 million lbs/year (deficit covered by inventories).

The Bloomberg Commodity Index (BCOM) weights: energy ~30%, grains ~23%, precious metals ~19%, industrial metals ~16%, softs ~7%, livestock ~5%. BCOM uses a production-weighted methodology capped at 33% per sector. S&P GSCI is more energy-heavy at ~55% weighting, making it more volatile.

Seasonal spread trades in commodities exploit calendar patterns: buy July natural gas / sell January (summer injection vs winter withdrawal), or buy July soybean / sell November (old crop vs new crop). These spreads have lower margin requirements (50-75% less than outright positions) and more predictable patterns.

Copper mine supply takes 5-10 years from discovery to first production due to permitting, construction, and infrastructure. Current global copper reserves are ~880 million tonnes with annual production ~22 million tonnes. The energy transition (EVs need 3-4x more copper than ICE vehicles, plus grid buildout) may create a 5-10 million tonne annual deficit by 2030.

Commodity options use Black-76 model (not Black-Scholes) because futures, not spot, are the underlying. Call option on WTI at $80 strike with 30-day expiry, 30% implied vol, costs approximately $2.50/barrel ($2,500 per contract). At-the-money straddle premium in crude oil roughly equals daily_range * sqrt(DTE) * 0.4.
